name: Pumpkin Chocolate Chip Muffins
recipeIngredient: Make one or two days ahead - for best flavor.
1 cup (half of a 1 lb can) plain pumpkin
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Toast almonds on a baking sheet or in a toaster oven for about 5 minutes, just until lightly browned. Watch carefully so they don't burn.
3. Remove toasted almonds and let them cool.
4. Grease muffin cups with butter or use foil/paper baking cups.
5. In a large bowl, mix together flour, sugar, pumpkin pie spice, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
6. In another bowl, beat eggs and add pumpkin and melted butter until well blended.
7. Stir in chocolate chips and toasted almonds.
8. Pour the wet ingredients over the dry ingredients and fold with a rubber spatula until just combined.
9. Scoop batter evenly into muffin cups.
10. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, or until puffed and springy when gently touched in the center.
11. Remove from oven and let cool on a wire rack. Store in an airtight container for up to 1-2 days. Reheat before serving.
